title: SIBD-63: A Dataset of A-Level Cross-Domain Structural Isomorphism Discoveries with Shared Equations and Variable Mappings
description:
63 cross-domain structural isomorphism discoveries produced by three independent AI-assisted pipelines (V1 broad-recall embedding, V2 strict-precision embedding, V3 StructTuple + LLM rerank) operating on a 4,443-phenomenon natural-language knowledge base. By construction the pipelines have zero overlap at the A-rated tier; each contributes 24, 19, and 20 records respectively.

Each record pairs two phenomena from disjoint scientific domains that share the same underlying mathematical structure, and is annotated with a proposed shared equation (for 20 records), variable mapping, suggested target journal, literature-gap assessment, and a step-by-step empirical execution plan. Records span 48 domain labels across geology, condensed-matter physics, ecology, molecular biology, financial microstructure, decentralized finance, behavioral economics, civil engineering, pharmacology, and more.

Released as a seed bank for empirical follow-up papers: domain experts can pick a seed matching their expertise and convert it into a publishable paper in 3-6 months. See the companion paper for methodology details and the README for usage and collaboration instructions.

Companion empirical validations covering five of the SOC-class records (earthquakes, S&P 500 returns, DeFi liquidations across Aave V2 + Compound V2 + MakerDAO, mouse neural avalanches, and null controls) are available at https://structural.bytedance.city.
notes: Companion code and full pipeline source at https://github.com/dada8899/structural-isomorphism . V1 and V2 trained embedding models available on Hugging Face at https://huggingface.co/qinghuiwan.
references: Wan Q. 2026. Recovering SOC Universality on a Global Earthquake Catalog. Layer 5 Phase 1 companion paper, Structural Isomorphism Project.
Wan Q. 2026. Cross-Protocol SOC Universality in DeFi Liquidation Cascades: 43,065 Events Across Aave V2, Compound V2, and MakerDAO. Layer 5 Phase 3 companion paper.
Clauset A, Shalizi CR, Newman MEJ. 2009. Power-law distributions in empirical data. SIAM Review 51, 661.
